⚙️  TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS

JWT_SECRET:
  • Size: 64 characters \(512 bits\)
  • Encoding: Base64
  • Generation: crypto.randomBytes\(48\).toString\('base64'\)
  • Security: Meets NIST requirements for cryptographic keys

Environment Validation:
  • JWT_SECRET minimum: 32 characters \(256 bits\)
  • JWT_SECRET maximum: unlimited
  • POSTGRES_URL: required to be set
  • NODE_ENV: must be 'development', 'production', or 'test'

Dev Server:
  • Validation runs on startup
  • Exits with code 1 if validation fails
  • Provides detailed error messages
  • No silent failures possible

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
                          HOW SESSION WORKS NOW
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

SIMPLE APPROACH \(Uses Browser Cookies Naturally\)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Login:
  1. User enters credentials
  2. POST /api/auth/login
  3. Backend sets auth_token cookie \(HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Strict\)
  4. Redirect to dashboard.html
  5. ✅ LOGGED IN \(cookie stored in browser\)

Navigation:
  1. User on dashboard.html
  2. Click any link
  3. Browser AUTOMATICALLY sends auth_token cookie with request
  4. Navigate to any page
  5. Cookie still exists \(untouched\)
  6. ✅ STILL LOGGED IN

Session Persistence:
  1. Cookie sent with every request \(automatic browser behavior\)
  2. Expires in 24 hours \(Max-Age=86400\)
  3. NO code needed to maintain session
  4. Browser handles it automatically
  5. ✅ SESSION PERSISTS AUTOMATICALLY

Logout:
  1. Click "Выйти" button
  2. Dashboard.js: handleLogout\(\) → POST /api/auth/logout
  3. Backend sets cookie Max-Age=0 \(expires immediately\)
  4. Browser deletes the cookie
  5. Redirect to login page
  6. ✅ LOGGED OUT


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
                            KEY INSIGHT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

The browser handles session persistence AUTOMATICALLY via cookies.

Complex auth-state.js was trying to replicate what browsers do natively.

Now we're using standard HTTP + cookies pattern = simpler, more reliable.
